handoff_goal: Specify workflows as phase execution contracts in cc-jiangxia, including grouped phase fields, soft-by-default constraint semantics, recommended phase skill bindings, dependency-aware sharing, lifecycle/completion status rules, and runtime/editor UI behavior.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-001 workflows as phase execution contracts
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Workflow contract semantics are reduced to cosmetic editor grouping.
Lifecycle or UI controls are split away and lose consistency.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-003 grouped semantics with session-owned runtimeState
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Runtime state becomes editable template data.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-004 constraint strengths guidance policy evidence gate
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Every instruction becomes a hard machine gate.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-005 recommended skills bind to existing skills and remain soft
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Plugins become the primary target or recommended skills auto-run.
Audit lists every recommended skill mechanically or records nothing.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-007 dependency manifest sharing without default bundles
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Import silently drops missing references or export implies bundled skills.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-008 lifecycle status separate from completion status
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Normal blocked/unable outcomes become terminal runtime failures.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-009 pending confirmation Confirm Reject Retry
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Pending ready submissions can be overwritten silently.
UI mixes manual completion with pending confirmation or uses auto-advance as a button.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-011 compatibility-aware adapter before direct grouped persistence migration
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Implementation requires destructive migration without fixtures.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-012 scheduler auto-execution required gates bundles destructive migration non-goals
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Feature expands into a scheduler or capability engine.

source_signal_disposition.signal: MP-013 live evidence authoritative while cognition is partial

source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Workflow skills degrade to prompt text only.

source_signal_disposition.source: UI discussion and live editor/API reads
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: Authoring is reduced to manual copy-only templates.

source_signal_disposition.signal: full workflow scheduler or execution engine
source_signal_disposition.reopen_trigger: User explicitly asks workflows to become the central scheduler.
User explicitly requests runtime-driven skill execution.
Reviewed bundle mode is requested with security review.

must_preserve.claim: Specify workflows as phase execution contracts, not only prose templates or phase skill hints.
Unified scope includes grouped fields, constraints, phase skills, sharing, lifecycle, UI, compatibility, and validation.
Use intent, contract, evidencePolicy, and session-owned runtimeState as grouped semantics.
Constraints are guidance, policy, evidence, and gate; hard gates stay sparse and explainable.
Recommended phase skills bind to existing skills, not plugins, and are soft by default.
Soft audit records used or clearly relevant skipped/unavailable recommended skills only.
Workflow sharing uses template plus dependency manifest by default.
Lifecycle status and completion submission outcome are separate.
Pending confirmation resolves only through confirm, reject, or retry and blocks duplicate ready submissions.
Runtime controls distinguish pending confirmation, manual completion, blocked/unable retry, auto-advance label, and session-level cancel/resume.
First scope is compatibility-aware and should adapt existing flat fields before direct grouped persistence migration.
Do not build a full workflow execution engine, scheduler, or default auto-executing skill system in this scope.
Live repository evidence is authoritative because project cognition was stale/unavailable.

consequence_gate.trigger_reason: Workflow contracts affect lifecycle operations, running workflow sessions, prompt behavior, SkillTool permission-sensitive behavior, shared skill/catalog state, import/export compatibility, final reports, and user-visible transition controls.

consequence_obligations.claim: Define stable workflow skill binding identity across local, bundled, plugin, managed, and MCP sources.
Define priority precedence without overriding system, developer, security, or explicit user safety boundaries.
Define behavior for missing, stale, disabled, invalid, ambiguous, plugin-disabled, unsupported-source, or unavailable workflow skills.
Preserve explicit permission handling for skill-derived tools, shell expansion, hooks, forked agents, model changes, and effort changes.
Snapshot or otherwise preserve workflow skill provenance for running, pending, completed, resumed, stale-template, and missing-template sessions.
Make workflow skill use and skip/unavailable decisions observable to the user and downstream artifacts.
Validate workflow skill bindings in workflow template authoring, import/export, duplicate/update, and session start paths.
Define UI state labels and affordances for authoring and runtime workflow contracts.
Preserve workflow skill evidence, completion evidence, and transition provenance through compaction, resume, final reports, and imports.
Prevent recursive or unbounded nested skill invocation.
